question_text: Explain why comets and asteroids have different compositions and why comets develop tails.
correct_answer: Asteroids are rocky bodies formed in the inner solar system. Comets are icy bodies from the outer solar system that develop tails when they approach the Sun because solar heat vaporizes their ice and solar wind pushes the gas and dust away from the Sun.
explanation: Asteroids are mainly rock and metal, while comets are 'dirty snowballs' of ice, dust, and rock. When a comet gets close to the Sun, the heat causes ice to sublimate (turn to gas), creating the characteristic glowing tail that always points away from the Sun due to solar radiation pressure.
